app.title: Paperless IQ
app.subtitle: Ask your archive anything
analysis.title: Document Search & Analysis
analysis.searchFailed: Search failed. Check Paperless NGX connection.
analysis.noMetadata: No metadata could be determined for this document.
analysis.newHint: New — will be created if "Create missing values" is checked
analysis.paperlessUnavailable: Cannot connect to Paperless NGX. Make sure PAPERLESS_URL and PAPERLESS_TOKEN are configured.
queue.title: Approval Queue
queue.emptyConfirmDetail: This will reject all {count} pending suggestion(s). This cannot be undone.
discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.subtitle: Ask questions about your documents. Answers are grounded in your actual document content with citations.
discovery.placeholder: Ask a question about your documents…
discovery.emptyHint: e.g. "What invoices did I receive from Amazon in 2024?"
processing.title: Processing Pipeline
processing.embedPausedBody: Embedding stopped after repeated failures and is waiting for you to fix the cause. Check the embedding provider and model in Settings → AI Provider, then save to resume. The error from the provider was:
processing.trackingHint: "Tracked" documents are ones the inbox monitor has seen. They won't be re-analyzed unless you reset the tracking. Rejected documents stay tracked unless explicitly reset.
processing.confirmResetAll: ⚠️ Reset all tracking? All inbox documents will be re-analyzed on the next poll cycle.
processing.confirmResetRejected: ⚠️ Reset {count} rejected documents? They will be re-analyzed on the next poll cycle.
settings.title: Settings
vision.optionsDescription: Send the document pages as images to the LLM for visual analysis.
vision.includeContentDescription: Extract the full text from the images and offer to replace the OCR content in Paperless.
vision.ollamaVisionWarning: Warning: the configured Ollama model may not support vision. Results may be incorrect.
vision.pageLimitWarning: This document has {count} pages. Sending all pages may incur significant LLM costs (threshold: {threshold} pages).
vision.pageLimitCostNote: Choose how many pages to send to the LLM.
vision.analyzingDescription: Sending document images to the LLM. This may take a moment.
vision.contentDiffDescription: Compare the original OCR text with the text extracted from the document images. Click Replace to update the content in Paperless NGX.
vision.noContentNotice: The model returned no document text. Metadata suggestions were still applied. Try a higher render DPI, fewer pages, or a stronger vision model.
queue.applyContent: Apply suggested content to the document
common.policy.allowNew: Allow new — keep unknown {entity}, create automatically
login.subtitle: Sign in with your Paperless-NGX account
appearance.navIconsHint: Type a Tabler icon name (e.g. file-text, bell). Leave blank to keep the default.
connection.title: Paperless NGX Connection
connection.publicUrl.label: Public URL
connection.publicUrl.description: The URL your browser uses to reach Paperless NGX. Used for 'Open in Paperless' links. Leave empty to fall back to the internal PAPERLESS_URL.
connection.inboxTag.label: Documents with this tag are picked up for processing
connection.inboxTag.error: Cannot load tags from Paperless NGX.
connection.internalUrl.label: Paperless IQ internal URL
connection.internalUrl.description: How Paperless NGX reaches Paperless IQ on the internal network. Leave empty to use the URL detected from the browser request.
aiProvider.llm.title: Language Model (LLM)
aiProvider.provider.label: Provider
aiProvider.model.label: Model
aiProvider.ollama.url.label: Ollama Server URL
aiProvider.ollama.url.description: The URL of your Ollama instance. No API key needed.
aiProvider.bedrock.region.label: AWS Region
aiProvider.bedrock.accessKeyId.label: Access Key ID
aiProvider.bedrock.secretKey.label: Secret Access Key
aiProvider.bedrock.sessionToken.label: Session Token
aiProvider.bedrock.sessionToken.descript: Permanent IAM access keys don't need this. Required only when keys came from AWS SSO / sts assume-role / sts get-session-token. Encrypted at rest using SECRET_KEY.
aiProvider.bedrock.sessionToken.placehol: Leave blank for permanent IAM user keys
aiProvider.apiKey.label: API Key
aiProvider.apiKey.description: Encrypted at rest. Leave empty to keep existing credentials.
aiProvider.openai.baseUrl.label: Custom Base URL
aiProvider.openai.baseUrl.description: Leave blank for the default OpenAI API. Set to a custom URL for OpenAI-compatible providers such as Open WebUI, LM Studio, or a self-hosted Ollama OpenAI endpoint (e.g. https://your-server/v1).
aiProvider.contextWindow.label: Context Window (characters)
aiProvider.contextWindow.description: Maximum characters sent to the LLM. Default: 128,000.
aiProvider.visionThreshold.label: Vision Page Warning Threshold
aiProvider.visionDpi.tip: Resolution for rendering document pages to images before full-document analysis. Higher = sharper text (better transcription) but larger images and more tokens. 150 is a good balance; raise to ~200 for dense/small text.
aiProvider.visionDpi.label: Vision Render DPI
aiProvider.visionThreshold.description: Show a cost warning when a document has more pages than this before vision analysis.
aiProvider.llmTimeout.label: LLM Timeout (s)
aiProvider.llmTimeout.tip: Maximum seconds to wait for an LLM response in Discovery. Set to 0 for no limit. Increase for large local models — a 70B model on CPU can take several minutes.
aiProvider.embeddings.title: Embeddings
aiProvider.embeddings.subtitle: Used for semantic search and smart entity selection. Can use a different provider than the LLM.
aiProvider.embeddings.provider.label: Embedding Provider
aiProvider.embeddings.provider.descripti: Provider used to generate document embeddings.
aiProvider.embeddings.ollama.hintPre: Make sure the model is pulled:
aiProvider.embeddings.ollama.hintPost: Embedding models are small and fast — independent of the LLM.
aiProvider.embeddings.model.label: Embedding Model
aiProvider.embeddings.model.description: Ollama model used for document embeddings. Must support the embed API.
aiProvider.embeddings.concurrency.label: Parallel Embeddings
aiProvider.embeddings.concurrency.tip: Number of chunks embedded concurrently. Default 1 is safe for a local Ollama instance. Increase to 4–8 when Ollama runs on a remote or GPU-backed host to speed up indexing.
aiProvider.embeddings.concurrency.tipClo: Number of embedding API calls in flight at once, across all documents. Bedrock handles parallel calls well — 8 is a good starting point. Lower it if you hit throttling, raise it for faster indexing.
aiProvider.embeddings.concurrency.descri: Higher values index faster but increase the request rate. 8 is recommended for Bedrock.
aiProvider.embeddings.batchSize.label: Embedding Batch Size
aiProvider.embeddings.batchSize.tip: Texts sent per embedding API call. Only Cohere Embed models on Bedrock batch multiple texts in one call (up to 96), which cuts request count and cost. Titan ignores this and always sends one text per call.
aiProvider.embeddings.batchSize.descript: Cohere only — fewer API calls per document. No effect on Titan models.
aiProvider.embeddings.ollama.urlEmbed.de: URL of your Ollama instance for embedding generation.
aiProvider.embeddings.bedrock.model.desc: Uses the same AWS credentials as your Bedrock LLM. Changing the model requires re-indexing — use Re-index on the Processing page after saving.
aiProvider.embeddings.bedrock.titanV2: Titan Embed Text v2 — 1024-dim, better quality (recommended)
aiProvider.embeddings.bedrock.cohereMult: Cohere Embed Multilingual v3 — best for non-English documents
aiProvider.embeddings.bedrock.cohereEn: Cohere Embed English v3 — best for English-only archives
aiProvider.embeddings.bedrock.titanV1: Titan Embed Text v1 — 1536-dim, legacy (keep if already indexed)
aiProvider.embeddings.openai.hint: Uses the same API key and base URL as your OpenAI LLM. The default model (text-embedding-3-small) works for standard OpenAI and most compatible providers.
aiProvider.vectorStore.title: Vector Store
aiProvider.vectorStore.backend.label: Backend
aiProvider.vectorStore.kbId.label: Bedrock Knowledge Base ID
aiProvider.vectorStore.kbId.placeholder: Only needed for Bedrock KB backend
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantMode.label: Qdrant Mode
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantMode.tip: Local: connect to a Qdrant container on your own infrastructure. Cloud: connect to Qdrant Cloud with an API key.
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantUrl.label: Qdrant URL
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantUrl.tip: URL of your Qdrant instance. Default http://qdrant:6333 matches the Docker Compose service name.
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantApiKey.tip: Required for Qdrant Cloud; leave blank for local instances. Stored encrypted.
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantApiKey.desc: Encrypted at rest — never returned to the UI.
Name of the Qdrant collection that stores document chunks. Changing this requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.vectorStore.qdrantMemoryColle: Name of the Qdrant collection that stores Discovery conversation memories.
aiProvider.vectorStore.backend.tip: Choose where document embeddings are stored. Local (ChromaDB) is the default and works without extra infrastructure. Qdrant offers tunable HNSW recall. Bedrock KB delegates to Amazon.
aiProvider.vectorStore.needsReindexDefau: The backend changed. Migrate existing embeddings or start a full re-index.
aiProvider.vectorStore.reindexReason.emb: Embedding model changed. Existing vectors are stale and may have a different dimension. Re-index the vector store to rebuild with the new model.
Embedding model changed alongside the backend; existing vectors can't be reused. Re-index to populate the new store.
aiProvider.vectorStore.reindexReason.no_: No existing store to migrate from. Re-index to populate the new backend.
aiProvider.vectorStore.reindexReason.bac: The vector backend changed. Re-index to populate the new store.
aiProvider.vectorStore.reindexReason.chu: Chunk settings changed. Existing documents keep their old chunk structure until re-indexed.
aiProvider.vectorStore.migrateFailed: Migration failed — try a full re-index.
aiProvider.vectorStore.migrateNeedsReind: Migration couldn't carry all vectors over — a re-index is needed.
aiProvider.search.title: Similarity Search Tuning
aiProvider.search.overfetch.label: Overfetch Multiplier
aiProvider.search.overfetch.tip: Candidate chunks fetched = top_n × this. Higher values improve recall at the cost of more post-processing.
aiProvider.search.minScore.label: Min Score
aiProvider.search.minScore.tip: Drop results whose normalised cosine score is below this threshold. 0 disables the filter.
aiProvider.search.chunkSize.label: Chunk Size (chars)
aiProvider.search.chunkSize.tip: Maximum characters per embedded chunk. Smaller = more precise retrieval; larger = more context per chunk. Changing this requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.search.chunkOverlap.label: Chunk Overlap (chars)
aiProvider.search.chunkOverlap.tip: Characters shared between consecutive chunks. Overlap ensures answers that straddle a boundary are still retrieved. Changing this requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.search.chunkStrategy.label: Chunk Strategy
aiProvider.search.chunkStrategy.tip: char: fixed-width character windows (default, fast). sentence: packs whole sentences up to chunk_size — avoids mid-sentence cuts. Changing this requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.search.reindexHint: Changing this requires re-indexing existing documents.
aiProvider.search.chromaSearchEf.label: Search ef
aiProvider.search.chromaSearchEf.tip: HNSW query candidate list size. Higher = better recall, more latency. Must be ≥ similar_docs_count × overfetch. Applied live — no re-index needed.
aiProvider.search.chromaM.label: M (graph connectivity)
aiProvider.search.chromaM.tip: Number of bidirectional links per node in the HNSW graph. Higher = better recall, more memory. Build-time — requires re-indexing to take effect.
aiProvider.search.chromaConstructionEf.t: Index build quality. Higher = better recall at the cost of slower indexing. Build-time — requires re-indexing to take effect.
aiProvider.search.qdrantEf.label: HNSW ef
aiProvider.search.qdrantEf.tip: Query-time candidate list size. Higher = better recall, more latency. Must be ≥ similar_docs_count × overfetch.
aiProvider.search.qdrantM.label: M (graph connectivity)
aiProvider.search.qdrantM.tip: HNSW graph connectivity. Build-time — requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.search.qdrantQuantization.tip: Compress vectors to save memory. none: full precision. scalar: 4× compression, minimal quality loss. binary: 32× compression, noticeable quality loss. Requires re-indexing.
aiProvider.search.qdrantHybrid.label: Hybrid search (dense + sparse)
aiProvider.search.qdrantHybrid.tip: Combines dense semantic vectors with sparse keyword (BM25) matching via RRF fusion — improves recall for precise/rare-term queries. Needs the fastembed sparse encoder (paperless-iq[qdrant-hybrid]) and a reindex to take effect.
aiProvider.search.rerankEnabled.label: Enable re-ranking
aiProvider.search.rerankEnabled.tip: After vector search, re-score (query, passage) pairs with a cross-encoder. Significantly improves precision. Adds latency proportional to rerank_top_k × passage length.
aiProvider.search.rerankMethod.label: Reranker
aiProvider.search.rerankMethod.tip: llm: listwise prompt to your configured LLM (zero new deps). local: in-process cross-encoder (optional sentence-transformers dep, ~1 GB model downloaded on first use). api: Bedrock Rerank (reuses Bedrock credentials).
aiProvider.search.rerankTopK.label: Re-rank top K
aiProvider.search.rerankTopK.tip: Number of vector-search candidates passed to the reranker. Higher = better recall before reranking, more reranker latency.
aiProvider.search.rerankModel.label: Model
aiProvider.search.rerankModel.tip: HuggingFace model ID for the local cross-encoder, or Bedrock model ID/ARN for the API method.
aiProvider.search.rerankModel.descriptio: BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3 is recommended for multilingual corpora (German, French, etc.). ~560 MB, downloaded on first use.
aiProvider.search.rerankApiDescription: For the Bedrock Rerank API, enter a Bedrock rerank model ID or ARN (e.g. amazon.rerank-v1:0 or cohere.rerank-v3-5:0). Uses your configured Bedrock credentials — no separate key needed.
automation.enable.description: Automatically poll for new documents with the inbox tag and analyze them in the background.
automation.autoApply.label: Auto-apply suggestions (skip approval queue)
automation.autoApply.description: AI suggestions are applied immediately without human review. Combined with "Allow new" creation policies, this will create new tags, correspondents, and types automatically.
automation.pollInterval.label: Poll Interval (seconds)
automation.batchSize.label: Batch Size
automation.cron.label: Cron Schedule
automation.cron.description: Optional cron expression to trigger processing on a fixed schedule.
automation.policies.title: Creation Policies
automation.policies.description: Controls whether the LLM can suggest values that don't yet exist in Paperless NGX. "Existing only" filters them out; "Allow new" keeps them and creates the entity when the suggestion is applied.
automation.policies.alert: Only relevant when auto-apply is enabled. For manual review, new values are always created when you approve. With auto-apply and "Allow new", entities are created automatically without review.
metadataRules.title: Smart Entity Selection
metadataRules.description: When enabled, Paperless IQ finds processed documents similar to the one being analyzed and sends only their tags, correspondents, and types to the LLM as candidates. This reduces prompt size and significantly improves suggestion accuracy.
metadataRules.similarDocs.label: Similar documents to consider
metadataRules.similarDocs.description: How many similar processed documents to draw entity candidates from.
metadataRules.frequencyFallback.label: Frequency fallback count
metadataRules.frequencyFallback.descript: Top-N most-used entities added as fallback (handles cold-start and rare categories).
prompts.title: System Prompt
prompts.global.label: Global prompt — system instruction sent to the LLM with every document
prompts.global.hint: Use {{content}} as placeholder for document text.
prompts.outputLang.label: LLM Output Language
prompts.outputLang.placeholder: e.g. de, fr, es (leave empty for English)
prompts.outputLang.description: Language the LLM should use for metadata values (title, tags, etc.). Leave empty for English.
prompts.fields.title: Field Instructions
prompts.fields.description: Give the LLM specific instructions for each metadata field. Leave blank to let it decide based on the system prompt alone.
prompts.fields.customFieldsError: Cannot load custom fields from Paperless NGX.
prompts.discovery.title: Discovery Prompt
prompts.discovery.label: System prompt for document discovery (RAG)
prompts.discovery.placeholder: Leave empty to use the built-in default prompt.
prompts.discovery.hint: The language rule ("Always answer in …") is prepended automatically based on the LLM Output Language setting above. Leave empty to use the built-in default.
memories.title: Long-term Memory
memories.enable.description: When enabled, key facts are automatically extracted from Discovery conversations and injected as context in future chats. Facts are deduplicated — similar entries are merged rather than duplicated.
memories.noMemories: No memories yet. Facts will appear here after Discovery conversations are closed.
acl.settings.title: Access Control Settings
acl.settings.description: When sync is enabled, Paperless NGX admins (superuser / staff) automatically receive full Paperless IQ access without needing manual permission grants.
acl.settings.syncLabel: Sync Paperless NGX admins — grant them full PIQ access automatically
acl.users.noUsers: No users found. Users appear here after their first login, or when Paperless NGX is connected.
acl.users.noRecordTooltip: No PIQ record yet — permissions take effect on first login
acl.users.footer: Users marked no record exist in Paperless NGX but have not yet logged into PIQ. Permissions you set here take effect on their first login. Deleting a record revokes all access — the user can log in again to create a fresh record with default (deny-all) permissions.
acl.audit.description: Controls how long audit log entries are retained before automatic deletion.
acl.audit.retention.label: Retention period (days, minimum 30)
acl.audit.retention.description: Default: 180 days (6 months). Entries older than this are deleted automatically.
acl.maintenance.description: Administrative actions that rebuild internal state. Use with care.
acl.maintenance.reindexAllDesc: Wipes and rebuilds the vector store from scratch. Required after changing the embedding model.
acl.maintenance.reindexSinceLabel: Re-index documents modified on or after:
acl.maintenance.resetDesc: Reset forces all documents to be re-analyzed on the next automation cycle.
discovery.suggestions.contracts: What contracts are currently active and when do they expire?
discovery.suggestions.noticePeriod: Which documents mention a notice period or cancellation clause?
discovery.suggestions.paymentTerms: What are the payment terms in my contracts?
discovery.suggestions.penalty: Find documents that mention a penalty or liability clause
discovery.suggestions.insurance: What insurance policies do I have and what do they cover?
common.title: Title
audit.table.title: Title
